description: Cutover inventory for optional legacy JSON/menu cleanup after accepted RmlUi parity. Cleanup remains separately reviewable and does not gate normal RmlUi menu operation.

cutover_gate.notes: No item may move to ready or complete until the parity manifest has parity_ready route coverage and all required evidence categories are complete.

targets.notes: Primary current JSON menu definition; roadmap baseline reports 56 named menus across the active JSON menu footprint.
Multiplayer/session JSON menu definition that must remain until RmlUi parity is accepted.
Current JSON menu loader/parser surface; revisit only after RmlUi documents and controllers replace all current JSON menu behavior.
Legacy reference content retained for behavior audit before final cleanup.
Server browser behavior must be replaced by a live RmlUi controller before removal.
Demo browser behavior must be replaced by a live RmlUi controller before removal.
Player model/skin/weapon preview behavior must be replaced by a live RmlUi controller before removal.
Shared list/feed behavior must either move behind explicit RmlUi controllers or remain intentionally retained.
Guarded RmlUi open path currently preserves legacy fallback; do not remove fallback until runtime parity is accepted.
Current runtime probe/scaffold must be replaced by first-class runtime behavior before fallback cutover.
Public RmlUi runtime bridge declarations need final ownership review before legacy bridge cleanup.
Packaging currently stages both new RmlUi assets and existing assets; final cleanup must be gated by parity and install validation.
Local distributable staging root must be refreshed after any final asset removal.
Round-specific validation artifacts remain scratch-only and are not final cutover output.
Developer roadmap must record final deletion/archive rationale and accepted parity evidence.
Canonical strategic roadmap must reflect completion or intentional archive status.
End-user docs need practical workflow updates if any menu behavior or visible wording changes.
OpenGL-native live route evidence is required before removal.
Vulkan-native live route evidence is required before removal; Vulkan must not fall back to OpenGL.
RTX/vkpt live route evidence is required before removal.
Screenshot/layout parity evidence is required before removal.
Escape/Back behavior must be verified before removing legacy fallback.
